what_it_does: Renders a static design-system preview card showing five branded box and vessel variants: circle, octagon, hatched hexagon, stage card, and tag, with labels, roles, SVG styling, and token notes.
observations: Includes a dsCard metadata comment naming the preview "Card / box variants" in the Brand group with a 700x260 viewport.
Imports shared card styling from _card.css and defines file-local CSS for layout, typography, shapes, metadata, and token footer.
Uses inline SVG definitions for hatch pattern, warm stacked shadow, and paper/cream gradients.
Displays five shape examples in a five-column rail: portal circle, hub octagon, product-module hexagon, process-stage rounded rectangle, and inline-caption tag.
Each example pairs a visual shape with a name and uppercase role label.
Includes a token footer documenting stroke, fill, hatch, shadow, and ink choices.
The tag example uses inline style attributes for its dimensions and text styling.
standouts: The shape vocabulary is encoded directly as SVG geometry with shared defs for fill, hatch, and shadow.
The file presents semantic role mapping beside each vessel shape rather than only showing visual samples.
The token footer turns the visual treatment into reusable implementation cues.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties for typography and most colors.
Keeps the preview self-contained with local styles and inline SVG assets.
Uses uppercase micro-labels for roles and token labels.
Uses repeated cell structure for each variant.
Breaks the local class pattern with inline styles on the tag shape and tag text.
concerns: The fixed five-column grid and 170px stage card may overflow in narrower containers.
Some tag-specific styling is inline, making that variant less consistent with the rest of the CSS.
notes: A compact brand-system specimen for shape-based product, portal, process, and label containers.
questions: Should the tag variant have a named CSS class like the stage card instead of inline styles?
Is the five-column rail intended only for the declared preview viewport, or should it respond to smaller widths?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it previews branded vessel variants and documents their visual tokens.
intention_signals: It seems intended to define a reusable visual language where different container shapes communicate different ecosystem or product roles while sharing one branded material treatment.
novelty: The hatched hexagon and role-specific shape taxonomy give the preview a distinct substrate-led component vocabulary.
contribution: Contributes a file-local vocabulary of branded container shapes, role labels, SVG treatments, and tokenized styling cues that a fused system would keep as neutral design substrates.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML design-system preview of five branded card and box variants using SVG shapes, gold strokes, cream fills, warm shadows, labels, and token notes.
intention_for_embedding: Created to demonstrate how shape, fill, stroke, hatch, shadow, and typography can encode distinct branded vessel roles in the ConceptV design system.
